# Project Guidelines

## Overview

WhatsApp Business frontend. Displays conversations and messages received/sent via the Meta Cloud API, with real-time updates via Supabase Realtime. Authenticated users can view all their conversations and will eventually be able to reply.

## Stack

- **Runtime**: Browser + Node.js (SSR/SSG), deployed via `waku build` / `waku start`
- **Framework**: Waku 0.27.4 — React Server Components. Entry point is `src/pages/_layout.tsx`
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4 (Vite plugin, no config file)
- **Database / Auth / Realtime**: Supabase JS ^2.87.3 — anon key + user JWT (RLS enforced)
- **Types**: Generated from Supabase — run `npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id <ref> > src/types/database.types.ts` after any schema change
- **Linter**: ESLint 9 + typescript-eslint + eslint-plugin-react-hooks (`npm run lint`)

## Architecture

```
src/
  pages/
    _layout.tsx                   # Root layout (wraps every page)
    index.tsx                     # Home / landing
    conversations.tsx             # /conversations — thin RSC wrapper, renders <ConversationsChat />
    whatsapp-numbers.tsx          # /whatsapp-numbers
    link-whatsapp-account.tsx     # /link-whatsapp-account
    about.tsx
    privacy-policy.tsx
    auth/
      callback.tsx                # Supabase OAuth callback handler
  components/
    header.tsx / footer.tsx / navigation.tsx / layout-client.tsx
    redirect-client-if-logged.tsx
    auth/
      email-login-form.tsx        # Sign in / sign up form
    facebook/
      accounts-table.tsx          # WhatsApp Business Accounts list
      numbers-table.tsx           # Phone numbers list
      embbed-signup-button.tsx    # Meta Embedded Signup flow
      login-button.tsx
    google/
      login-button.tsx
    conversations/
      chat.tsx                    # Orchestrator — all state, effects, real-time subscriptions
      chat-utils.ts               # Shared types + text extraction helpers
      conversation-sidebar.tsx    # Left panel: search, conversation list, status dots
      message-panel.tsx           # Right panel: header, message bubbles, reply bar
  lib/
    supabase.ts                   # All Supabase client calls — single client, anon key + RLS
    facebook.ts                   # Meta Graph API helpers ('use server')
    useAuth.ts                    # useAuth() hook — returns { user, loading }
    sync-numbers.ts               # Syncs Meta phone numbers into Supabase
  types/
    database.types.ts             # Auto-generated — DO NOT edit manually
    facebook.d.ts                 # Meta webhook / API payload types
  styles.css                      # Global styles (Tailwind import)
  pages.gen.ts                    # Auto-generated by Waku — DO NOT edit manually
public/
  images/                         # Static assets
```

## Key Conventions

### Waku pages
- Full Waku documentation for AI: https://waku.gg/llms.txt
- File-based routing lives in `./src/pages`. Every page and layout exports a default React component and a named `getConfig` that returns `{ render: 'static' }` (SSG) or `{ render: 'dynamic' }` (SSR). `'static'` is the default for pages and layouts.
- **Layouts**: `_layout.tsx` files wrap the route and all descendants. Must accept `children: ReactNode`. Root layout is `./src/pages/_layout.tsx`.
- **Segment routes**: `[slug].tsx` → receives `slug` as prop. Nested: `[category]/[product].tsx`. Catch-all: `[...catchAll].tsx`. Static segment routes require a `staticPaths` array in `getConfig`.
- **Group routes**: directories named `(group)` organise routes without affecting URLs — useful for scoping layouts.
- **API routes**: files in `./src/pages/_api/` export named HTTP method handlers (`GET`, `POST`, etc.) or a default catch-all. Dynamic by default; can export `getConfig` with `render: 'static'` for static resources.
- **Ignored directories**: `_components` and `_hooks` inside `pages/` are not routed.
- **Client components**: mark with `'use client'` at the top of the file. This creates a server–client boundary — all components imported below the boundary are hydrated in the browser. Never mix server and client logic in the same file.
- **Server actions**: mark with `'use server'` inside a function body (inline) or at the top of a file (marks all exports). This is **not** the same as a server component — do **not** place it at the top of RSC files.
- **Navigation**: use `<Link to="...">` from `waku` for internal links; `useRouter()` hook for programmatic navigation (`router.push`, `router.replace`, `router.back`, etc.).
- **Environment variables**: server-side use `getEnv('VAR_NAME')` from `waku`; client-side use `import.meta.env.WAKU_PUBLIC_*` (must have `WAKU_PUBLIC_` prefix to be public).
- Path aliases are configured in both `tsconfig.json` and `waku.config.ts`: `components/`, `lib/`, `pages/`, `types/`.

### TypeScript
- `strict: true`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true` — all enabled.
- Never use `any`. Cast unknown JSON fields via explicit type guards.
- Optional properties must explicitly include `| undefined` in their types (required by `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`).

### Styling
- Tailwind CSS v4 only — no separate `tailwind.config.js`.
- JSX string literals use single quotes (`'use client'`, classNames with single quotes).
- ESLint enforces `quotes: single` and `jsx-quotes: prefer-single`.

### Supabase client (`src/lib/supabase.ts`)
- `src/types/database.types.ts` is **auto-generated** — never edit it manually. After any schema change run: `npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id <ref> > src/types/database.types.ts`
- Single exported `supabase` client using the **anon key** — user JWT is applied automatically via `supabase.auth` and RLS is enforced.
- All DB functions live here. Do not call `supabase` directly from components.
- Fetch functions return `{ data: data ?? [], error }` — never throw, always return the error.
- `wpp_messages.direction` is either `'inbound'` or `'outbound'`.
- **24h window**: query `wpp_active_conversations` view — never compute it manually in frontend code. The view exposes `is_active` and `last_inbound_at`. Use `dbGetActiveConversationIds()` which returns a `Set<string>` of currently open conversation IDs.
- Types: `Database['public']['Tables']['wpp_messages']['Row']`, `Database['public']['Views']['wpp_active_conversations']['Row']`, etc.

### Real-time (`src/components/conversations/chat.tsx`)
- Realtime requires `supabase.realtime.setAuth(session.access_token)` before subscribing (called once on mount).
- Channel naming: `wpp:conversation:${id}:messages` with `{ config: { private: true } }`.
- Use `broadcast` events (`INSERT`, `UPDATE`) — **not** `postgres_changes`.
- Subscribe to **all** loaded conversations at once (not just the selected one) so unread badges work for background conversations.
- Use functional state updaters (`setSelectedId(current => ...)`) inside broadcast handlers to avoid stale closures.
- Clean up all channels in the `useEffect` return: `channels.forEach(ch => supabase.removeChannel(ch))`.

### Conversations UI
- `chat.tsx` is the sole owner of all state: `conversations`, `messages`, `selectedId`, `unreadIds`, `activeIds`, `loadingConversations`, `loadingMessages`.
- `ConversationSidebar` and `MessagePanel` are pure presentational — they receive props and callbacks only.
- `chat-utils.ts` holds all shared types (`Conversation`, `Message`, `LastMessagePreview`) and pure helpers (`getMessageText`, `getPreviewText`, `formatTime`, `formatConversationDate`, `getInitials`). No React imports here.
- Message bubble direction: `direction === 'outbound'` → right-aligned green bubble (`bg-[#d9fdd3]`, `rounded-tr-none`); `'inbound'` → left-aligned white bubble (`rounded-tl-none`).
- Template messages (`message_type === 'template'`) render a blue "TEMPLATE" badge above the text.
- `activeIds` (from `dbGetActiveConversationIds()`) drives the window dot in the sidebar and the "Janela aberta / encerrada" pill + warning banner in the panel. Refreshed on mount and on every incoming broadcast.

### Meta / Facebook (`src/lib/facebook.ts`)
- Marked `'use server'` — never imported in client components.
- Uses `getEnv('FACEBOOK_SYSTEM_USER_SECRET_TOKEN')` via Waku's `getEnv` (from `waku/server`).
- All Graph API calls go through `fbFetch(endpoint, options)` which prepends the base URL and Authorization header.
- Media URLs (`image`, `audio`, `video`, `document`, `sticker`) are stored directly in `wpp_messages.message_content.url` by the backend — no need to call the Graph API from the frontend to resolve them.

### Auth
- `useAuth()` hook (`src/lib/useAuth.ts`) — returns `{ user: User | null, loading: boolean }`. Uses `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange` internally.
- `redirect-client-if-logged.tsx` — client component that redirects authenticated users away from auth pages.
- OAuth callback is handled at `src/pages/auth/callback.tsx`.

## Build & Dev

```bash
npm install       # install dependencies
npm run dev       # waku dev — local dev server
npm run build     # waku build — production build
npm run start     # waku start — serve production build
npm run lint      # eslint . — lint all files
npm run lint:fix  # eslint . --fix — auto-fix
```

## Schema changes

After any Supabase schema migration:
```bash
npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id <ref> > src/types/database.types.ts
```
`src/types/database.types.ts` and `src/pages.gen.ts` are **auto-generated** — never edit them manually.
